options: A: Follow design doc complex interfaces
B: Follow Card A.1 spec refined fields
rationale: Card A.1 spec is the refined version of design doc interfaces; later cards can extend schemas as needed
residual_risk: Design doc has richer types (e.g. reviewerVerdict as object) that may need schema updates in later cards

options: A: god required in SessionMetadata (breaking for existing sessions)
B: god optional in SessionMetadata (backward compatible)
rationale: Existing persisted sessions lack god field; making it optional ensures backward compatibility on resume. cli.ts uses fallback: god ?? reviewer
residual_risk: Legacy sessions without god will default to reviewer on resume

options: A: Only check absolute paths
B: Resolve relative paths to cwd then compare against ~/Documents
rationale: Card A.4 spec explicitly requires relative path resolution to cwd (AC-028); resolve() handles both cases correctly
residual_risk: Symlinks could bypass the check; acceptable for v1

options: A: Block all curl commands
B: Only block curl with -d/@file data exfiltration patterns
rationale: Design doc specifies 'curl -d @file patterns'; blocking all curl would break legitimate API calls
residual_risk: Other exfiltration patterns (wget, nc) not covered; acceptable scope for initial 5 rules

options: A: Template-based with {{placeholder}} resolution like ContextManager
B: Section-based builder with typed PromptContext
rationale: Section-based approach is simpler, avoids template file I/O, and naturally enforces FR-003b priority ordering through section append order. ContextManager's template approach is retained as fallback (AR-004).
residual_risk: No custom prompt template support from .duo/prompts/ — acceptable for God-orchestrated sessions where God controls prompt content

options: A: Throw error on extraction failure
B: Fallback to safe default (continue_to_review for PostCoder, route_to_coder for PostReviewer)
rationale: Safe defaults maintain workflow continuity per FR-G01 degradation strategy. continue_to_review is the 95% case for PostCoder; route_to_coder with generic issue is safe for PostReviewer.
residual_risk: Fallback may miss Coder crash detection; acceptable since retry logic exists at higher level

trigger: AC-018b enforcement strategy for empty unresolvedIssues
options: A: Reject and re-query God
B: Inject generic issue and proceed
rationale: Re-querying God adds latency and may fail again. Injecting a generic issue satisfies AC-018b constraint while keeping workflow moving. The generic message instructs Coder to review feedback.
residual_risk: Generic issue is less specific than God-extracted issues; Coder may miss specific fixes

options: A: Increment round in EVALUATING only (existing behavior)
B: Also increment round in ROUTING_POST_REVIEW → ROUTE_TO_CODER transition
rationale: When God directly routes from POST_REVIEW to CODING (bypassing EVALUATING), the round must still increment to prevent infinite loops and track progress correctly.
residual_risk: Dual increment paths exist (EVALUATING and ROUTING_POST_REVIEW); callers must not use both for same round

options: A: Extend existing ConvergenceLogEntry in god-prompt-generator.ts
B: Define new richer ConvergenceLogEntry in god-convergence.ts
rationale: Card B.3 spec defines a richer ConvergenceLogEntry (with classification, blockingIssueCount, criteriaProgress, summary) than the existing simpler one in god-prompt-generator.ts. Creating a new interface in the convergence module preserves backward compatibility (AR-004) while satisfying AC-020.
residual_risk: Two ConvergenceLogEntry types exist; callers must use the correct one for their context

options: A: Reject and re-query God on inconsistency
B: Override shouldTerminate to false on inconsistency
rationale: Re-querying God adds latency and may produce the same hallucination. Overriding to the conservative path (do not terminate) is safer and consistent with FR-G02 semantic contradiction handling: 'use countable fields as authority source, auto-correct'.
residual_risk: God's other judgment fields (classification, reason) remain as-is even when shouldTerminate is overridden

options: A: Keep existing RECLASSIFY mapping from Card B.2
B: Add dedicated PHASE_TRANSITION event with nextPhaseId+summary payload
rationale: Card B.4 spec explicitly requires 'phase_transition → 触发 XState PHASE_TRANSITION event'. A dedicated event type allows carrying phase transition payload (nextPhaseId, summary) which RECLASSIFY cannot. RECLASSIFY event retained in machine for backward compatibility.
residual_risk: Two event types (RECLASSIFY and PHASE_TRANSITION) both route to WAITING_USER; callers must use the correct one

options: A: Any single signal triggers loop detection
B: Require multiple corroborating signals (stagnant trend + non-decreasing blockingIssueCount)
rationale: AC-032 requires low false positive rate. Requiring corroborating signals (trend labels + actual blockingIssueCount data) reduces false positives. If blockingIssueCount is actually decreasing, we override stagnant trend labels.
residual_risk: Very conservative detection may miss edge case loops where issues change but count stays same

options: A: Track retries separately per error kind (L2 retry counter, L3 retry counter)
B: Use single consecutiveFailures counter with odd/even for retry/fallback cycle
rationale: Single counter is simpler, naturally reaches L4 threshold (3) after mixed error sequences. Odd failures trigger retry, even failures trigger fallback. Matches design doc's 'consecutive 3 L2/L3 → L4' requirement without separate tracking.
residual_risk: Alternating retry/fallback means 2nd failure always falls back even if error kind changed; acceptable since fallback is safe.

trigger: GodDecision union type for checkConsistency input
options: A: Accept only GodConvergenceJudgment
B: Accept union of GodConvergenceJudgment | GodPostReviewerDecision via type guards
rationale: Card C.2 spec requires checking confidenceScore < 0.5 (PostReviewerDecision field) AND approved+blockingIssueCount (ConvergenceJudgment). Union type with runtime type guards covers both schemas without breaking existing code. Type guards use structural checks (key presence) which are reliable for discriminated objects.
residual_risk: If future God schemas share overlapping field names, type guards may misidentify; acceptable since schemas are controlled by us.

options: A: god_too_permissive as mild (warning only)
B: god_too_permissive as severe (fallback activated)
rationale: 3 consecutive God-local disagreements indicates systematic drift, not noise. Severe classification with 2-round fallback prevents compounding bad decisions while auto-recovery limits disruption.
residual_risk: Fallback may be unnecessarily activated for benign 3-streak edge cases; acceptable since auto-recovery is fast.

options: A: All confidence_declining as severe
B: Severity based on final confidence score (< 0.5 severe, >= 0.5 mild)
rationale: A declining trend from 0.9 to 0.6 is less concerning than 0.8 to 0.2. Using the final score as severity discriminator aligns with LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD from consistency-checker.ts and preserves proportional response.
residual_risk: Threshold boundary (exactly 0.5) is mild; may want to tune later.

trigger: God state placement in SessionState vs separate interface
options: A: Create separate GodSessionState interface stored in a parallel field in SessionSnapshot
B: Extend SessionState with optional God fields (godSessionId, godAdapter, godTaskAnalysis, godConvergenceLog)
rationale: SessionState already has optional coderSessionId/reviewerSessionId pattern. Adding God fields to the same interface preserves the single snapshot.json write path, reuses existing atomic write and validation infrastructure. No need for a parallel persistence path.
residual_risk: SessionState interface grows; acceptable since all God fields are optional and backward-compatible.

options: A: Truncate entire prompt at MAX_PROMPT_CHARS
B: Truncate individual sections (coder/reviewer output) with per-section limits, then overall cap
rationale: Per-section truncation (MAX_OUTPUT_SECTION_CHARS=15000 each) ensures both coder and reviewer outputs are proportionally represented before the overall 40k char (10k token) cap. Pure overall truncation could cut one section entirely.
residual_risk: Fixed per-section limit may not be optimal for all output size distributions; acceptable for v1.

options: A: Rebuild only when tokenEstimate >= limit (100%)
B: Rebuild at 90% threshold to prevent quality degradation before exhaustion
rationale: FR-012 says 'context窗口耗尽(导致输出质量下降)' — rebuilding at 90% prevents quality degradation rather than waiting for complete exhaustion. 90% is conservative enough to avoid premature rebuilds.
residual_risk: 90% threshold may trigger slightly early for adapters with good degradation handling; acceptable since rebuild preserves continuity.

options: A: Accept full SessionState + SessionMetadata (couples to session internals)
B: Accept TriPartySessionState + minimal RestoreConfig (decoupled)
rationale: Decoupled interface only requires session IDs and adapter names per role. Avoids coupling restore logic to SessionState internal structure. extractTriPartyState bridges the gap.
residual_risk: RestoreConfig is a subset of SessionConfig; callers must provide correct adapter names.

options: A: latencyMs required (breaking existing callers)
B: latencyMs optional (backward compatible)
rationale: Existing callers of appendAuditLog (god-router, god-convergence, drift-detector, degradation-manager, god-prompt-generator) do not provide latencyMs. Making it optional preserves backward compatibility per AR-004.
residual_risk: Some audit entries may lack latencyMs; acceptable since it is supplementary metadata.

options: A: GOD_ERROR as Warning (non-blocking)
B: GOD_ERROR as Critical (blocks workflow)
rationale: Design doc FR-021 explicitly specifies GOD_ERROR as Critical level with system message. AC-054 requires Critical alerts to pause workflow for user confirmation.
residual_risk: All God API failures block workflow; benign transient errors may over-block. Acceptable since DegradationManager handles retries before alerts surface.

trigger: TASK_TYPE_LIST ordering for number key mapping
options: A: Order alphabetically
B: Order by frequency/importance (explore, code, discuss, review, debug, compound)
rationale: Ordering by common usage puts the most likely selections (code, explore) at keys 1-2. Matches TaskTypeSchema enum order from god-schemas.ts.
residual_risk: Users may expect alphabetical order; acceptable since number keys are a shortcut, not the primary interaction.

options: A: Include all 6 TaskTypes including compound and discuss
B: Exclude compound (UI mockup shows only 4 types: code/explore/review/debug)
rationale: Design doc FR-002a UI mockup explicitly lists only 4 types. compound is a meta-type that requires phases definition, and discuss is not listed in the mockup. Excluding compound prevents semantically undefined reclassification. discuss excluded per mockup.
residual_risk: Users cannot reclassify to compound or discuss mid-session; acceptable since compound requires phases and discuss is rare.

options: A: Throw error on extraction failure
B: Fallback to request_human (safest default)
rationale: request_human is the safest fallback for WAITING_USER auto-decision — it defers to human rather than taking potentially wrong autonomous action. Consistent with god-router.ts fallback pattern.
residual_risk: Extraction failure always requires human intervention; acceptable since it indicates God output quality issues.

options: A: Dynamic width based on terminal columns
B: Fixed 50-char width for consistency
rationale: Fixed width ensures consistent visual appearance across terminals and simplifies line width equality testing. Terminal-responsive sizing can be added later if needed.
residual_risk: Content may truncate on very narrow terminals; acceptable for God messages which are brief summaries.

options: A: Create separate GodOverlayType (no extension of existing)
B: Extend existing OverlayType union with god in both overlay-state.ts and keybindings.ts
rationale: Extending existing OverlayType preserves the single overlay state management pattern (only one overlay open at a time). Ctrl+G follows the same toggle pattern as Ctrl+I/Ctrl+T. Adding god to the union is backward compatible.
residual_risk: Both overlay-state.ts and keybindings.ts define OverlayType separately; they must stay in sync. Acceptable since this is an existing pattern.
